name: AD:VENTURE Grant
summary: Grant for early-stage businesses in West Yorkshire within their first 3 years of trading. Covers growth-related costs including equipment, software, marketing, and professional services. No repayment required.

eligibility.criterion: Registered and operating in West Yorkshire
Trading for under 3 years at point of application
Fewer than 10 employees
Some professional services sectors excluded — confirm with provider

restrictions: Cannot be used to pay the business owner's salary
Cannot be used to repay existing debt
Budget is finite and allocated annually — not guaranteed year-round

name: Start Up Loans
summary: Government-backed personal loan for businesses under 3 years old. Fixed 6% annual interest rate with 12 months interest-free at the start. Includes 12 months of free mentoring.

eligibility.criterion: UK-based business
Trading for under 3 years at point of application
Personal credit check required

restrictions: Maximum £25,000 per individual applicant
Personal liability — loan is made to the individual, not the company
Repayments begin after the 12-month interest-free period

name: Help to Grow: Digital

funding.display: 50% discount on approved software, up to £5,000

summary: Government-funded 50% discount on approved digital software — covering accounting, CRM, e-commerce, and productivity tools. Applied at point of purchase from an approved supplier, not reimbursed afterwards.

eligibility.criterion: UK-based business
Fewer than 250 employees
Trading for over 12 months

documents: Companies House or HMRC registration number
restrictions: Only valid for software on the government-approved catalogue
Discount applied at purchase — not a cash payment or reimbursement
One redemption per business

name: WY Growth Fund
summary: Grant for growing businesses in West Yorkshire looking to scale, create jobs, or expand capacity. Aimed at businesses with a proven model and demonstrable growth trajectory rather than early-stage startups.

eligibility.criterion: Registered and operating in West Yorkshire
Demonstrated trading history and revenue
Evidence of growth potential or job creation
Typically expects turnover of £50,000 or above — confirm with provider

documents: Most recent filed accounts or management accounts
Evidence of demand (e.g. confirmed orders, waiting list, contracts)
restrictions: Cannot be used for general working capital or day-to-day running costs
Spend must be tied to a specific growth or job-creation objective

name: Leeds City Council Business Grants
summary: Leeds City Council offers a range of grant schemes for local businesses, with eligibility and funding amounts varying by programme. Schemes typically prioritise local job creation, neighbourhood economic development, and support for underrepresented founders.

eligibility.criterion: Based and operating in Leeds
Eligibility criteria vary by individual scheme — check current availability

restrictions: Scheme availability and terms vary — restrictions depend on the specific grant applied for

timeline.open: Varies — check Leeds City Council website for live schemes

name: Creative Places Growth Fund
summary: Grant fund supporting businesses in West Yorkshire's creative industries. Aimed at growing the region's creative economy — arts, media, design, film, music, and related sectors.

eligibility.criterion: Registered and operating in West Yorkshire
Operating in the creative industries sector (arts, media, design, film, music)
Growth-oriented with a clear development objective

documents: Portfolio or evidence of creative output
Description of intended spend and growth objective
restrictions: Restricted to creative industries — hospitality, retail, and general services are not eligible
Spend must support the creative output or growth of the business

timeline.open: Check provider for current application windows

name: Help to Grow: Management
summary: Subsidised 12-week management and leadership programme delivered by UK business schools. Covers strategy, finance, marketing, and operations. Government covers 90% of the cost; businesses pay £750.

eligibility.criterion: UK-based business
Trading for at least 1 year
5 or more employees
Senior manager or director applying (not a sole trader without staff)

documents: Proof of employment (payroll records or HMRC submissions showing 5+ employees)
Companies House or HMRC registration number
restrictions: Programme fee of £750 payable by the business — not fully free
Applicant must be a senior manager or decision-maker within the business
Time commitment of approximately 50 hours over 12 weeks

timeline.decision: Rolling intake — cohort start dates vary by business school

name: Business Enterprise Fund
summary: Community Development Finance Institution offering loans to businesses in Yorkshire and the Humber that may not qualify for high-street bank lending. Covers asset finance, working capital, and growth investment.

eligibility.criterion: Based in Yorkshire and the Humber
Typically requires at least 12 months of trading history
Must demonstrate ability to repay
Viable business plan required

documents: Most recent filed accounts or management accounts
restrictions: Loan product — full repayment required with interest
Interest rates vary depending on loan size and risk profile
Personal guarantee may be required for larger loan amounts

name: Business & IP Centre Leeds
provider: Leeds Central Library / British Library Network
summary: Free business support, advice, and resources available at Leeds Central Library. Part of the British Library's Business & IP Centre network. Includes access to market research databases, one-to-one advisor sessions, workshops, and IP guidance.

eligibility.criterion: Open to anyone starting or growing a business in Leeds

restrictions: Not a funding programme — no cash or discount provided
Some workshops and one-to-one sessions require advance booking

timeline.decision: Immediate access for drop-in resources; bookable sessions within 1–2 weeks

name: King's Trust Enterprise Programme
summary: Low-interest loan and mentoring programme for young people aged 18–30 starting or growing a business. Includes business planning support, a dedicated mentor, and ongoing advice.

eligibility.criterion: Aged 18–30 at point of application
eligibility.notes: owner_age must be collected during onboarding — not available from HMRC or Companies House

eligibility.criterion: UK-based business
Early-stage or pre-start business

documents: Proof of age (passport or driving licence)
restrictions: Strictly age-gated — applicants must be 30 or under
Loan product — repayment required, though at low interest rates
Maximum loan of £4,000

name: Innovate UK R&D Grants
provider: Innovate UK / UK Research and Innovation
summary: Competitive grant funding for businesses developing novel technology, products, or processes. Requires genuine research and development activity. Highly competitive — applications are assessed against innovation merit, commercial potential, and team capability.

eligibility.criterion: UK-based business
Project must involve genuine R&D — developing something novel, not applying existing knowledge
Must demonstrate commercial potential of the innovation
Typically requires matched funding or industry collaboration for larger awards

documents: Letters of support from partners or customers (where applicable)
restrictions: Funding is for R&D activity only — not for general trading, marketing, or operational costs
Projects must be time-limited with defined milestones
Subject to grant monitoring and reporting requirements

timeline.open: Competitive rounds — check Innovate UK Funding Finder for open calls

name: R&D Tax Relief

funding.display: Tax credit or reduction on qualifying R&D expenditure

summary: HMRC scheme allowing companies to claim tax relief on qualifying research and development costs. Can result in a reduction in corporation tax or a cash credit for loss-making companies. Claimed through the annual corporation tax return.

eligibility.criterion: UK limited company subject to corporation tax
Must have incurred qualifying R&D expenditure in the tax year
R&D activity must seek to achieve a scientific or technological advance

documents: Detailed description of R&D projects and qualifying activities
Breakdown of qualifying expenditure (staff costs, materials, software, subcontractors)
restrictions: Only available to limited companies — sole traders cannot claim
Standard design, marketing, and business development work does not qualify
Claims are subject to HMRC review and may be investigated

timeline.decision: Processed as part of corporation tax return — typically 4–8 weeks after submission
timeline.open: Claimed annually via corporation tax return
